whatWasImplemented: supabase/migrations/20260419040000_match_chunks_file_sources.sql — recreates match_chunks() (via DROP + CREATE because return-shape changed) to include file-sourced embeddings and expose source_type in the result. File chunks (source_type='file') are NOT subject to the crawl-batch swap gate — they persist across crawls by design. Crawl chunks (source_type='crawl' or legacy NULL) still gate on active_crawl_batch.

src/lib/chat/system-prompt.ts — SystemPromptChunk now carries an optional source_type. Citations render as '[N] (File: <filename>)' for file-sourced chunks (detected by source_type='file' OR source_url starting with 'file://') and '[N] (Source: <url>)' for crawl chunks. Legacy rows without source_type still render correctly.
src/__tests__/system-prompt-files.test.ts — 3 tests: file citations formatted, legacy file:// recognized without source_type, crawl chunks unchanged.
whatWasLeftUndone: Widget-side visual distinction (document icon vs link icon for citations) — M5F6 UI is pure-server for this feature; widget follow-up deferred because the widget hasn't surfaced citation chrome historically.
End-to-end test: upload a PDF with unique content, ask a question, assert the widget answer cites it. Deferred to Playwright (VAL-FILE-024 flagged as pending there).

chat/session route still passes the chunks through unchanged — it already forwards source_url, and now source_type flows alongside thanks to the updated match_chunks return. No call-site change required.
discoveredIssues: Postgres refuses CREATE OR REPLACE when a function's OUT columns change — migration has to DROP first. Named the drop explicitly with all arg types (vector, text, uuid, double precision, integer) to avoid ambiguity.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 426 passed / 1 skipped / 57 files (+3 new).

manualChecks.action: match_chunks SQL applied with new return columns
manualChecks.expected: no migration error; subsequent runs idempotent
